content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 18, 2003


John Green
8797 Oak Drive
San Miguel, CA 93451

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-03-005

Dear Mr. Green:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Consistent with
the discussions held on March 14, 2003, we are withdrawing your letter.

     In your letter, you stated that you are currently a second term member of
the San Miguel Joint Union School District Board of Trustees and it is your intent
to seek a seat on the San Miguel Community Service District Board ("CSD") in
the 2004 election. You are concerned that you may not be able to hold both
offices simultaneously. As we noted, the "doctrine of incompatible offices" is not
a provision within the Act. Thus, the Commission does not advise on this
prohibition. You may be able to obtain information on this prohibition from
either the Attorney General's web site or by contacting their office directly.

     For your information we have enclosed two fact sheets, "Can I Vote?
Conflicts of Interest Overview" and "How Do I Get Advice From the FPPC?"
along with copies of two letters that pertain to holding multiple offices, and the
conflicts-of-interest rules of the Act (Yarborough Advice Letter, No. G-02-212;
Foster Advice Letter, No. A-02-125 ), which apply on a decision by decision
basis. If you should have a question under the Act about a particular decision in
the future, please contact us for advice.



_______________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2,
sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-03-005
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosures
JWW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\W-03-005

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a school district board member can simultaneously hold a seat on a community service district board without violating conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is withdrawing the advice letter because the doctrine of incompatible offices is not a provision within the Political Reform Act and falls outside FPPC's jurisdiction. The requestor should consult the Attorney General's office for guidance on this statutory prohibition.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (W-03-005). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present, but the document contains a clear question statement and the FPPC's rationale for withdrawal. References two related advice letters (Yarborough G-02-212 and Foster A-02-125) regarding multiple offices.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a school district board member can simultaneously hold a seat on a community service district board without violating conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is withdrawing the advice letter because the doctrine of incompatible offices is not a provision within the Political Reform Act and falls outside FPPC's jurisdiction. The requestor should consult the Attorney General's office for guidance on this statutory prohibition.

embedding.summary: FPPC withdrawal letter regarding a request about whether a school district board member can hold a community service district seat. The FPPC declined to advise because the incompatible offices doctrine is not within the Act's scope.
